Blueprint overview

01. Business formation

ASAM 0.5 through 4.0 — canonical reference.

Mental-health counterpart to ASAM — 6 levels.

Roles, ratios, hiring sequence, comp benchmarks.
◇

◇
09. Facility design
Real estate, zoning, ADA, occupancy, clinical space.
◇
10. Compliance foundations
HIPAA, 42 CFR Part 2, P&P manuals.
◇
11. Marketing & admissions
Referral sources, intake, VOB-to-admit, CARA §509.
◇
12. Outcomes measurement
What to track for payers and accreditation.

Why switch now

Every week on a legacy EMR
costs you.

Behavioral health operators face the worst margin pressure in a decade. Documentation eats clinician time. Burnout drives turnover. AI-native operators are pulling ahead. The cost of staying the same compounds.

2–4hrsper clinician per day

Documentation is killing your margin.

Clinicians on legacy EMRs lose 2–4 hours a day to charting. That is 10–20% of clinical revenue paying for paperwork instead of patient care. Navix gives that time back.

42%behavioral health turnover (2024 data)

Staffing pressure is at record highs.

Burnout, turnover, and hiring costs are punishing operators. Your platform has to be a force multiplier, not another reason your best clinicians quit. Navix removes the work clinicians hate.

Live · AI-native
Nowthe AI-native window

The AI shift is happening.

Operators adopting AI-native platforms move faster, run leaner, and capture more revenue. Operators who wait fall behind permanently. Navix is the only AI-native EMR built only for behavioral health.

See Navix on your data →Pricing

AI Tools Built In

AI · Built in, not bolted on
For treatment facilities

Built for the levels of care
that need real software.

Navix is purpose-built for behavioral health facilities — not solo therapists. Admissions, multi-location ops, utilization review, and the regulated complexity that comes with running a real treatment program.

01

Detox

Intake to stable handoff. Vitals, MAT, withdrawal protocols, discharge planning.

Learn more →
02

Residential Treatment

The full episode of care. Admissions, charting, treatment plans, group programming, family communication, discharge.

Learn more →
03

PHP / IOP

Group note generation, ASAM/LOCUS-aware UR, attendance tracking, and telehealth — built for high-volume group programming.

Learn more →
04

Outpatient Stabilization

Step-down outpatient with crisis-ready scheduling, medication management, and continuity into alumni programming.

Learn more →
Specialty coverage

Across every behavioral
health condition.

Built and validated for the full range of conditions facilities treat.

Mental HealthMood, anxiety, trauma, psychotic disorders
AddictionSUD, alcohol, opioid, stimulant, MAT
Eating DisordersAnorexia, bulimia, BED, ARFID
Dual DiagnosisCo-occurring mental health + SUD

Solo therapist or small group practice? See Navix Professional Edition →

Why Navix for facilities

Make the complex
simple.

We replace the 5–10 disconnected systems treatment facilities run with one unified platform — admissions to discharge, single location to network of 50.

  • Admissions to DischargeOne pipeline from inquiry through aftercare. VOB Agent included.
  • Multi-Location OperationsNetwork-wide dashboards, role-based access, consistent data across every site.
  • Compliance Built InASAM/LOCUS-aware UR, automated chart auditing, 42 CFR Part 2 ready.
  • AI Documentation + Agentic RCM (2027)NavixScribe writes the notes today; the Agentic RCM platform launching 2027 builds the claims from them and fights the denials.

What we are building · Preview summer 2026

An Autonomous Operating System for behavioral health facilities.

Navix AOS is the layer where documentation, compliance, scheduling, and ultimately billing run autonomously. AI does the volume work. Clinicians and operators supervise. AI is the tool. Humans are the moat.

Today's Navix is the foundation: CRM + EMR + 22 named AI agents, an open developer platform with a full REST API and MCP servers, and the operator-led product process that makes it work. AOS is what comes next, and only Navix has one.

Every release is shaped by what operators tell us they need. AOS is what separates Navix from every other behavioral health EMR on the market. Operators who get on early shape the platform.

Talk to the AOS team →Developer platform

The 12 phases

Blueprint overview
The full 12-phase plan in one page.
◇
01. Business formation
Entity, ownership, financial model, operating docs.
◇
02. State licensing
Application workflow, timelines, state-by-state friction.
◇
03. Accreditation
CARF vs Joint Commission — cost, timeline, payer fit.
◇
04. ASAM continuum
ASAM 0.5 through 4.0 — canonical reference.
◇
05. LOCUS framework
Mental-health counterpart to ASAM — 6 levels.
◇
06. Levels of care
Detox, RTC, PHP, IOP, OP, sober living — operationally.
◇
07. Staffing models
Roles, ratios, hiring sequence, comp benchmarks.
◇
08. Payer contracting
In-network vs OON, VOB, SCAs, payer mix.
◇
09. Facility design
Real estate, zoning, ADA, occupancy, clinical space.
◇
10. Compliance foundations
HIPAA, 42 CFR Part 2, P&P manuals.
◇
11. Marketing & admissions
Referral sources, intake, VOB-to-admit, CARA §509.
◇
12. Outcomes measurement
What to track for payers and accreditation.

Why switch now

Every week on a legacy EMR
costs you.

Behavioral health operators face the worst margin pressure in a decade. Documentation eats clinician time. Burnout drives turnover. AI-native operators are pulling ahead. The cost of staying the same compounds.

2–4hrsper clinician per day

Documentation is killing your margin.

Clinicians on legacy EMRs lose 2–4 hours a day to charting. That is 10–20% of clinical revenue paying for paperwork instead of patient care. Navix gives that time back.

42%behavioral health turnover (2024 data)

Staffing pressure is at record highs.

Burnout, turnover, and hiring costs are punishing operators. Your platform has to be a force multiplier, not another reason your best clinicians quit. Navix removes the work clinicians hate.

Live · AI-native
Nowthe AI-native window

The AI shift is happening.

Operators adopting AI-native platforms move faster, run leaner, and capture more revenue. Operators who wait fall behind permanently. Navix is the only AI-native EMR built only for behavioral health.

For treatment facilities

Built for the levels of care
that need real software.

Navix is purpose-built for behavioral health facilities — not solo therapists. Admissions, multi-location ops, utilization review, and the regulated complexity that comes with running a real treatment program.

01

Detox

Intake to stable handoff. Vitals, MAT, withdrawal protocols, discharge planning.

Learn more →
02

Residential Treatment

The full episode of care. Admissions, charting, treatment plans, group programming, family communication, discharge.

Learn more →
03

PHP / IOP

Group note generation, ASAM/LOCUS-aware UR, attendance tracking, and telehealth — built for high-volume group programming.

Learn more →
04

Outpatient Stabilization

Step-down outpatient with crisis-ready scheduling, medication management, and continuity into alumni programming.

Learn more →

What we are building · Preview summer 2026

An Autonomous Operating System for behavioral health facilities.

Navix AOS is the layer where documentation, compliance, scheduling, and ultimately billing run autonomously. AI does the volume work. Clinicians and operators supervise. AI is the tool. Humans are the moat.

Today's Navix is the foundation: CRM + EMR + 22 named AI agents, an open developer platform with a full REST API and MCP servers, and the operator-led product process that makes it work. AOS is what comes next, and only Navix has one.

Every release is shaped by what operators tell us they need. AOS is what separates Navix from every other behavioral health EMR on the market. Operators who get on early shape the platform.
